subject: Project Management
title: The Great Wall of China
description: A quick, practical guide to The Great Wall of China.

hook.text: The Great Wall stretches 21,000 km — built and rebuilt over 2,000 years by millions.

buildup.text: Different dynasties added sections using local materials: stone, brick, and tamped earth.

discovery.text: Workers were soldiers, peasants, and prisoners. Entire villages were conscripted for decades.

twist.text: The wall didn't stop invaders — Genghis Khan simply rode around it.

climax.text: As a construction project, it succeeded. As a military strategy, it mostly failed.

punchline.text: The world's longest project solved the wrong problem.

quiz.question: What was a key limitation of the Great Wall as a project?
options: It solved the wrong problem — invaders went around it
